title: Classic Bengali Sandesh

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Fresh lemon juice – 3 tbsp (or vinegar diluted with 1 tbsp water)
Powdered sugar (fine caster sugar) – 80–100 g (adjust sweetness)
Saffron strands – a few, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk
Rose water or kewra water – ½ tsp (optional, for aroma)
Edible silver leaf (varak) – optional, for luxury finish

instruction.title: Prepare the Chenna
steps: Boil the milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Once it reaches a rolling boil, reduce heat.
Stir in lemon juice gradually until the milk curdles completely and whey separates.
Strain through muslin/cheesecloth, rinse under cold water (to remove lemony taste).
Hang for 30 minutes to remove excess whey (should be moist, not dry).

instruction.title: Knead the Chenna
steps: Place the chenna on a clean surface and knead with the heel of your palm for 7–8 minutes, until smooth and soft (like a dough, no visible grains).
Add powdered sugar gradually, kneading further for 3–4 minutes until incorporated.
Mix in cardamom powder and saffron milk.

instruction.title: Cook the Mixture (Bhuno Process)
steps: Transfer chenna mixture to a nonstick pan.
Cook on low heat, stirring continuously, until mixture thickens slightly and leaves the sides (should remain moist, not dry).
This takes 5–7 minutes. Do not overcook, or it will become crumbly.

instruction.title: Shape & Garnish
steps: While still warm, divide into equal portions. Shape into smooth round or flat discs (traditionally using wooden moulds with floral designs).
Garnish with pistachios/almonds and apply silver leaf.

instruction.title: Serving Suggestion
steps: Pair with Rabri drizzle or fruit compote for a plated dessert in fine dining.

chefTips: Texture: Chenna must be silky; kneading is key.
Flavors: Infuse with saffron, rose, or even seasonal fruit (mango, strawberry puree) for gourmet variations.
Presentation: Serve on a chilled plate, garnished with silver leaf, microgreens, or edible flowers for a restaurant-style finish.
